commands.review_summary:
The PR reorders the Control UI Settings assistant avatar fallback so `chatAvatarUrl` is preferred before the assistant agent avatar route, and adds tests for that priority plus route fallback behavior.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +28. Total +30 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can pass `/avatar/<agentId>` directly to Quick Settings for local assistant avatars while gateway tests and docs show that route requires bearer auth. I did not run the full Tailscale browser flow.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus a code change to remove the protected-route fallback; because proof is missing, this should remain human-reviewed rather than routed to an automated repair marker.

target.title: fix: prioritize chatAvatarUrl over assistant agent route to avoid 401
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue where users who set an `Avatar` in their `IDENTITY.md` document would see a broken avatar image (401 response) in Settings → Personal → Assistant on the control-ui page. The avatar route built from the assistant agent ID was being preferred over the `chatAvatarUrl` provided by the profile document. ## Why This Change Was Made The `configAssistantAvatarUrl` fallback chain placed `state.chatAvatarUrl` (the avatar from `IDENTITY.md`) after `buildAssistantAvatarRoute`, so the agent-based route was always attempted first. Since that route returned 401 in the settings context, the avatar failed to display. The fix reorders the ternary so `chatAvatarUrl` is evaluated first, falling through to the agent route only when no profile avatar is set. ## User Impact Users who have configured an avatar in `IDENTITY.md` will now see it correctly rendered in Settings → Personal → Assistant instead of a broken image. No impact for users without a profile avatar — they continue to see the assistant agent route or default avatar as before. ## Evidence The change is a two-line reorder in the fallback chain: - Before: `buildAssistantAvatarRoute` was checked before `state.chatAvatarUrl`, causing a 401 for the agent-based route. - After: `state.chatAvatarUrl` is checked first, then falls back to the agent route or default. Closes #85750
target.author: leonidaslux
